summary: Iteration 3 testing of upgraded Contact page + Footer + backend contact model. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests passed including new full payload persistence (company/country/service/budget). Frontend: all requested testids present, availability badges (3), Let's Work (3 cards), 6 contact info cards, copy toasts appear, 9 quick actions, service dropdown lists 11 options (+1 placeholder = 12), Google Maps embed + open-maps, calendly placeholder, form validation without privacy blocks submit, checking privacy submits and shows animated success + Send Another returns to form, PT translation works fully, Footer social/whatsapp/cv-en/cv-pt/back-to-top all functional (CV endpoints return 200 application/pdf).
critical_code_review_comments: Clipboard copy in headless throws NotAllowedError (writeText). Toast still fires so UX is fine, but in dev the runtime error overlay renders. Consider wrapping navigator.clipboard.writeText in try/catch with an explicit fallback (execCommand or hidden textarea + select) and swallow the rejection to avoid the dev overlay noise.
Service dropdown has 12 <option> elements (1 disabled placeholder + 11 services). Matches requirement of 11 selectable services.
server.py is a single file (~290 lines) which is fine; contact model + PDF generator could be split into modules if it grows.
seed_data_creation: Test contact messages inserted via API (prefix TEST_).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Contact form testids: contact-name, contact-email-input, contact-message, contact-privacy, contact-submit, contact-success, contact-send-another. Native <select data-testid="contact-service"> includes 1 disabled placeholder + 11 real service options. Clipboard writes may throw in headless Chromium; treat toast appearance as success. Footer CV endpoints /api/cv?lang=en|pt return application/pdf.
